runs.userPrompt:
[page-annotation] Ant is annotating the live Metis Control Center UI.
Page: "Tasks" (mode id: tasks) — Full governed task board — project groups, search, filter, sort
Likely source files: projects/metis-command/components/tasks/TaskBoardMode.tsx
Repo: ${METIS_HOME}. Design rules: docs/design-guidelines.md (§6.5 iconography, §5 type, §6 spacing).
Attached image(s), if any, are screenshots of this page's current state — read them for the exact element Ant means.
If this needs a code change (formatting fix, new feature), COMPLETE the dispatch in THIS
turn — do not stop at a plan, do not reply "in-progress", do not wait for anything:
1) spawn_agents: ONE builder, kind codex or claude, in the metis-os workspace.
2) send_to_agent: the full scoped fix prompt to that agent — exact files above, what to
change, design-guidelines constraints, and "verify with npx tsc --noEmit + npm run lint,
then commit via scripts/git-lock.sh with an explicit pathspec, before reporting done".
3) Only then reply: what you dispatched, to which agent, and that Ant can watch in Agents.
A reply describing steps you have not executed is a failure.
If it is a question or trivially answerable, just answer.

Ant's annotation:
The search box placeholder on this page just says "search…" — change it to "search tasks…" so it is self-describing.

[page-annotation] Ant is annotating the live Metis Control Center UI.
Page: "Work Graph" (mode id: work-graph) — Tasks, blockers, milestones, goal progress, per-project status
Likely source files: projects/metis-command/components/workgraph/WorkGraphMode.tsx
Repo: ${METIS_HOME}. Design rules: docs/design-guidelines.md (§6.5 iconography, §5 type, §6 spacing).
If this needs a code change (formatting fix, new feature): spawn ONE tightly-scoped
builder agent (kind codex or claude) in the metis-os workspace whose prompt names the
exact files above, the design-guidelines constraints, and "verify with npx tsc --noEmit
+ npm run lint before reporting done". Reply with what you dispatched and where to watch.
If it is a question or trivially answerable, just answer.
